---
title: "Register-based research at DST"
subtitle: "Who is this guide for, and what will you learn?"
date: last-modified
---

This guide is for anyone working with register-based research at Statistics Denmark (DST).

::: {.callout-important}
**Assumptions** The examples assume your registers are stored as **parquet**, and they recommend **[fastreg](https://dp-next.github.io/fastreg/)** for reading a register by name (`read_register("bef")`). If your data is still in SAS, convert it once - see [Phase 4 - Convert SAS to parquet](04_filformater.qmd#convert-sas-to-parquet). Not using fastreg? The same parquet opens with `open_dataset()` (arrow), shown alongside throughout.
:::

---

## Where do you start?

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-4}
### New to register research?
Start at phase 1 and follow the phases in order - a little R experience makes the start easier.

→ [Start at phase 1](01_studieforberedelse.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Already know R?
Skip the introductory phases and go straight to the server, files and extractions.

→ [Jump to phase 3](03_log-ind-dst.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-4}
### Working on DARTER?
Project-specific setup steps and guidance.

→ [DARTER - overview and pipeline](darter/00_index.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.callout-tip}
**Looking for something specific?** Use the search box in the top right - it searches across the entire guide.
:::

---

## The phases of the guide

The guide is built as 14 phases. The roadmap shows the natural path through, from planning to export; the table below gives a quick overview. You don't have to read everything in order - the look-up pages (functions, pitfalls, overview of registers etc.) can be visited as you go.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {
  "themeVariables": {
    "fontSize": "24px"
  },
  "flowchart": {
    "nodeSpacing": 23,
    "rankSpacing": 40
  }
}}%%
flowchart LR
    S1["①  Get started<br>1 · 2 · 3"] --> S2["②  Get data<br>4 · 5 · 6 · 7 · 8"] --> S3["③  Build the study<br>9 · 10"] --> S4["④  Extract variables & assemble<br>11 · 12"] --> S5["⑤  Analyse & finish<br>13 · 14"]
    O1["Functions: overview"] ~~~ O2["Format tables"] ~~~ O3["DST pitfalls"] ~~~ O4["Overview of registers"] ~~~ O5["Good code practice"]
    O6["Algorithms & special packages"] ~~~ O7["Learning resources"] ~~~ O8["DARTER - Project 708421"]
    classDef stage fill:#eaf2fb,stroke:#4a78b5,stroke-width:1px,color:#173a5e;
    classDef ref fill:#f6f6f6,stroke:#aaaaaa,color:#555555;
    class S1,S2,S3,S4,S5 stage
    class O1,O2,O3,O4,O5,O6,O7,O8 ref
```

| Phase | Contents |
|---|---|
| [1 - Plan your study](01_studieforberedelse.qmd) | Research question, key concepts and data model |
| [2 - R: the bare essentials](02_r-intro.qmd) | The minimum of R you need to get going |
| [3 - Log in to DST](03_log-ind-dst.qmd) | Access to the server and the first overview |
| [4 - File types and loading](04_filformater.qmd) | Parquet and SAS - formats and conversion |
| [5 - Extracting data step by step](05_udtraek-trin-for-trin.qmd) | The universal extraction pattern: read_register/open_dataset → filter → collect |
| [6 - First extraction](06_foerste-udtraek.qmd) | Your first real extraction with synthetic data |
| [7 - Inspect your data](07_inspicer-data.qmd) | Check structure, types and distributions before analysis |
| [8 - Find your registers](08_find-dine-registre.qmd) | Find the right registers for exposure, outcome and covariates |
| [9 - Understand LPR](09a_forstaa_lpr.qmd) | LPR2/LPR3 and ICD codes |
| [10 - Build your study population](10_byg-studiepopulation.qmd) | Cohort, index date, in/exclusion, censoring and special designs |
| [11 - Extract variables](11_udtraek-variable.qmd) | Extract outcomes, socioeconomics and comorbidity - filtered to the cohort |
| [12 - Assemble and prepare the dataset](12_data-manipulation.qmd) | Joins, pivots and handling missing data |
| [13 - Analysis](13_analyse.qmd) | Tables, figures, regression, time-to-event, rates and sensitivity analyses |
| [14 - Export and repatriation](14_eksport-hjemsendelse.qmd) | Get your results safely out of DST |

### Look-ups - use as you go

These pages are **not** part of the numbered guide - jump here when you have a specific question.

| Page | Use it when you want to... |
|---|---|
| [Functions: overview](guide_til_funktioner.qmd) | look up a single function - what do `filter()`, `collect()`, `%>%`, `left_join()` do? |
| [Format tables](formateringstabeller.qmd) | translate codes to text (municipality, education and employment codes) with DST's SAS format files |
| [DST pitfalls](dst_faldgruber.qmd) | check the errors that most often cost time and give uninformative messages |
| [Overview of registers](register_reference.qmd) | find the confirmed column names, types and join keys for each register |
| [Good code practice](god-kode-praksis.qmd) | structure, naming and reproducible code |
| [Algorithms & special packages](pakker-oversigt.qmd) | ready-made algorithms (OSDC, NMI) to derive variables |
| [Learning resources](laeringsressourcer.qmd) | find courses, books and references for R, epidemiology and statistics |
